headline: Scientists create programmable heat material for smarter technology
meta_description: A new material can control and store thermal radiation without continuous power, opening doors to better sensors and energy devices.
summary_short: A breakthrough material can control heat like a computer program, remembering settings and directing thermal energy on demand.
body:
A newly developed material has cracked something that seemed impossible: the ability to program and control heat itself. The material can direct thermal radiation, switch between modes, and remember its settings without needing constant power—behaving almost like a computer program made of physics.

This breakthrough could ripple across multiple fields. Infrared sensors could become sharper and smarter. Energy technologies could work more efficiently. Even memory devices could eventually store information using light and heat instead of electrical charges. The innovation opens a new frontier in how materials interact with one of nature's most fundamental forces.
